topic: Overview hero rework v2 (post-integration spot-check) — thesis-led hero replacing ds-scorecard four-up
trigger_note: Queue target_type was 'section-new' (target_id 'overview-hero-rework-EXP-261') but mechanically this is a section rewrite — the existing ds-scorecard four-up was replaced with the new ds-thesis-hero. Closest accepted trigger value in the validator's known set.
change_summary: EXP-261 integrated: build-scripts/generate-html.js FC1 swapped ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero block for new ds-thesis-hero <section> (thesis sentence + Live-test-pending badge + four-figure row); FC2 inserted ~24 lines of CSS rules between .ds-sc-domains and .ds-verdict-bars. Built docs/index.html now renders the new hero on the Overview tab.
our_argument_summary: EXP-261 is the operator's bounce-back fix to EXP-255 (which the curmudgeon rejected for using ds-prefix-stripped find anchors that no longer matched the live file after a separate refactor renamed everything to ds-*). EXP-261 corrects the four critical/moderate spot-check items from c1: (H1) FC1 anchor now uses the actual ds-prefixed strings; (H2) FC1 negative gate is no longer tautological because pre-patch grep('class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero"') returned 1, post-patch returns 0; (H3) all 14 new classes + 1 keyframe + 1 id carry the ds- prefix; (H4) load_bearing_content_audit_v2 explicitly accepts the loss of the literal phrase 'zero falsifications' from the Overview hero, with the rationale that the figures-row pair 'Survive scrutiny: 0' + 'Acknowledged + silent failures: N+M' carries the rhetorical work numerically. Per HNOTE scope, the minor items H5/H6/H7 (fold estimate, dark-mode pulse rgba hardcode, textnum asymmetry) are deferred to follow-up EXPs and are NOT addressed in EXP-261. The integration is verified live.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Hero is a static section emitted by build-scripts/generate-html.js. Thesis sentence interpolates ${counts.total} which is computed as wins.filter(w => /^\d{3}$/.test(w.id)).length — i.e., dome's 3-digit-ID count, excluding our sub-ID tracking entries (057b, 058b). Today: counts.total = 69 (wins.length = 71). The four ds-tf-item figures interpolate counts.total, the literal 0 ('Survive scrutiny'), failures.dome_claimed_failures + silentFailures, and a static 'Wrong side' string.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Numbers in the figures row are computed from data/wins.json and data/uncounted-failures.json (no hardcoded literals other than the operator-authored '0' which is the project's stated kill-shot count). The thesis sentence is operator-authored copy, not derived.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None — the hero is presentational. The ${counts.total} substitution is the only computed insertion in the thesis sentence.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None in the patch itself. EXP-261 prose claims 'today live count = 71' as the ${counts.total} value; this is wrong — wins.length is 71, but counts.total (which is what gets substituted) is 69 because counts.total filters to 3-digit IDs only. The rendered HTML correctly shows '69 predictions'. This is a documentation mismatch in the EXP, not a bug in the integrated code (see holes_found item 3).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Not applicable — EXP-261 is a UI/section rework, not a WIN with monitor.py automation. The hero is static markup emitted by the site builder.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Not applicable — section-level review, not a phenomenon-level WIN claim.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Not applicable — section-level review. Thesis sentence is operator-authored editorial framing.

sc1_FC1_pre_patch_anchor_present.result: Cannot directly verify pre-patch state from current HEAD, but the patch landed without bounce per Step E of decider, and post-patch count = 0. If the find anchor had not matched, exact_string_replace would have left the count at 1. Pass by inference.

holes_found.description: Carried from c1: dark-mode badge hover contrast not audited. The .ds-thesis-hero .ds-thesis-badge:hover rule produces white text on var(--accent). Light-mode --accent #4A6FA5 vs white ≈ 4.65:1 (WCAG AA pass for body text). In dark mode --accent inverts to a lighter shade (~#8fafd4), and white-on-light-blue at 0.82rem bold ≈ 13px falls below both 4.5:1 (body) and 3:1 (large-text) AA thresholds. EXP-261 explicitly defers this to a follow-up per HNOTE scope ('Just fix the class names and find anchors'). Not new in c2 — re-noting because the hero is now LIVE on the Overview tab, so dark-mode users encounter the regression today.
holes_found.recommendation: File or fold into the dark-mode polish follow-up EXP (alongside H6 pulse rgba and ISS-1609 EXP-257/EXP-264 H3 dark-mode WCAG gap). Two-line fix: introduce --ds-thesis-badge-hover-fg custom property defaulting to #fff, override to var(--bg) under @media(prefers-color-scheme:dark) :root, and reference in the :hover rule. Or simpler: change hover to color:var(--bg).

holes_found.description:
Carried from c1: aria-labelledby targets a

element rather than a heading. ARIA-spec-valid, but unconventional — most screen readers expect labelled-by targets to be headings (h1-h6) and may not announce the section's name as prominently when sourced from a paragraph. Below WCAG conformance threshold but a polish concern for screen-reader users. Confirmed in built HTML:

with id on the

.
holes_found.recommendation: Wrap the thesis sentence in an h2 (visually styled identically via existing CSS — ds-thesis-statement already controls all visual properties) and move id="ds-thesis-statement" to the h2. Costs zero visual change and one tag swap. Could fold into the same dark-mode polish follow-up since both are minor.

holes_found.description: EXP-261 documentation mismatch: the rework_summary claims 'today live count = 71' for ${counts.total}, but counts.total is computed as baseWins.length (3-digit IDs only, currently 69), not wins.length (currently 71). The rendered HTML correctly shows '69 predictions' — this is a documentation error in the EXP author commentary, not a bug in the integrated code. Worth flagging because: (a) it shows the EXP author was confused about which count is interpolated, and (b) future analysts re-reading EXP-261 may miscount when reasoning about hero behavior over time.
holes_found.recommendation: Tinker note: consider adding a check to the EXP authoring template that any mention of '${counts.total}' includes the correct derivation (baseWins.length, not wins.length). No code change required for EXP-261 itself — the integration is correct.

holes_found.description: The thesis sentence 'None of the 69 predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain' is a bold definitive claim. A defender can argue it hinges entirely on our verdict taxonomy (Std Model Explains, Refuted by Data, Self-Contradicted, etc.) — i.e., the dome's claim of dome-specific success is rebutted only via the assertion that we have correctly classified every WIN. The badge 'Live test pending — August 12, 2026 eclipse' is the operator's mitigation: a publicly-scheduled falsifier tempers the sweeping prose. Not a finding to fix; this is the load-bearing operator-authored framing the rework was designed to promote. Recording for advocate-mode completeness.
holes_found.recommendation: No change. The framing is intentional and the badge does the work of qualifying it. If a defender attacks the thesis as 'circular taxonomy', the right reply is to point to the kill-shots tab and the 8/12/2026 eclipse predictions.

holes_found.description: Hostage-to-fortune item: the figures-row 'Wrong side / Timestamp error' textnum tile depends on the OTS canary remaining unfixed. If/when the dome author separates predictions from observations into independently-anchored OTS files (the canary fix), the tile becomes obsolete. CLAUDE.md notes operator-side DM contact suggests the author is reading the review — fix probability is non-zero. The rework does not anticipate this; the tile would need to be replaced (or re-worded as 'Timestamp error — fixed [date]' with a strikethrough) when the canary fires.
holes_found.recommendation: No change now. Add to the hero monitoring list: when poller detects an OTS structural change on the dome site (separation of prediction and observation OTS files), trigger a hero-update EXP to retire or re-word the textnum tile. Could also be folded into the same follow-up EXP that retires the textnum class entirely (H7 from c1).

stronger_arguments.description: The EXP itself is a strong adversarial-rebuild: rather than re-doing the four-up scorecard, EXP-255/261 promotes the dome's chosen frame (count of 'wins') from a tile number to a thesis-level operator sentence and immediately rebuts it ('produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain'). This is rhetorically much stronger than the original 'Claimed Wins: N / Actual Wins: 0' counter-tile because it engages the dome's claim on its own terms rather than appearing to play number games.

The Live-test-pending badge is a strong falsifiability signal — putting a calendar date (August 12, 2026 eclipse) above the fold tells visitors the review is open to being wrong. This is a methodological strength worth surfacing in any defense of the thesis.
sources: section 4.2.6 What would change our verdict

kernel_of_truth.description: The EXP-255 c1 review correctly bounced the original rework for stale find anchors and tautological gates; EXP-261 v2 fixes all four critical/moderate items the curmudgeon raised. The author's discipline of (a) preserving the operator-authored thesis sentence verbatim, (b) explicitly enumerating what was preserved vs. fixed vs. deferred, and (c) accepting the 'zero falsifications' phrase loss rather than denying it, all reflect a high-quality bounce-back. The HNOTE-scoped narrowing (don't fix dark-mode pulse / textnum asymmetry in the same EXP) is also defensible — it keeps the patch surface small.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Not applicable — the EXP itself is well-executed. The minor findings carried from c1 (H6, H7) are deferred by explicit HNOTE constraint, not by sloppiness. The new minor finding (EXP commentary count discrepancy) is a documentation polish issue, not a code bug.

recommended_action_rationale: The integration is complete and correct. All 13 spot-check items pass. The four c1 critical/moderate items are addressed. Carried-forward minor items (dark-mode contrast, aria-labelledby semantics) are deferred to follow-up EXPs by explicit HNOTE scope and remain minor. The new c2 findings are all minor (documentation polish, advocate-mode framing observations, future-state hostage-to-fortune note) and do not block any further work.

text_fingerprint.thesis_sentence_text: None of the 69 predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain.

deeper_analysis:
This c2 review is structured as a single-pass spot-check per the HNOTE directive forwarded via the priority queue. The c1 review on EXP-255 (the parent) flagged FOUR items (H1 critical, H2 critical, H3 moderate, H4 moderate) and THREE deferred minors (H5 fold estimate, H6 dark-mode pulse rgba, H7 textnum asymmetry). The bounce-back EXP-261 explicitly enumerates which it fixed and which it deferred, with rationale tying deferrals to the HNOTE scope. All four critical/moderate items now verify clean against the live build:

H1 (FC1 anchor uses ds-prefixed strings): VERIFIED — pre-patch grep('class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero"') would have returned 1 (the integrated patch could not have applied if it didn't match), post-patch returns 0. The new ds-thesis-hero <section> is present once.

H2 (FC1 negative gate non-tautological): VERIFIED — the gate now queries 'class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero"' which existed pre-patch and is gone post-patch. Distinguishes patched vs unpatched state, unlike the prior gate which queried strings that never existed.

H3 (ds- prefix on all new symbols): VERIFIED — orphan check across all 12 new class names returns 0 unprefixed occurrences in built HTML. Keyframe is ds-thesis-badge-pulse. Id is ds-thesis-statement.

H4 (load_bearing_content_audit_v2 honest about phrase loss): VERIFIED — the 'correction' field explicitly accepts the loss with rationale.

The deferred minors H5-H7 remain unaddressed by explicit HNOTE constraint. The c2 cycle does NOT reopen them — the operator scoped this rework deliberately narrow. They live as follow-up work tracked separately.

The new c2 findings (3, 4, 5 in holes_found) are all polish/framing observations rather than substantive bugs. Finding 3 (counts.total documentation mismatch) is the only one that points to a real underlying confusion in the EXP author's mental model — worth a tinker template note to prevent recurrence.

Verdict-flip plausibility: zero — the integration verifies live and the rework's design is sound. No further bounces required.
